goal: Generate a 100-frame PAIRED Synthetic Data Generation (SDG) dataset where every frame is rendered TWICE from identical object poses: once as a clean baseline (DR randomizers off / neutral lighting + neutral material) and once with a per-frame perturbation applied to lighting intensity and material color. The pair {frame_N_baseline.png, frame_N_perturbed.png} shares OBJECT POSES (same Cube positions and rotations within 1 mm tolerance across the two passes) and differs ONLY in the DR-controlled illuminant + material parameters, producing supervised pairs suitable for contrastive sim-to-real training or differential rendering loss. Scene: 4 labelled cubes scattered on a table under a DomeLight + single overhead camera. configure_differential_sdg(static_elements=['/World/Ground','/World/Table','/World/Cube_*'], dynamic_elements=['/World/DomeLight','/World/Cube_*/material'], randomize=['color','lighting']) declares pose-bearing prims as STATIC across passes and only lighting/material as DYNAMIC — this is the construct that guarantees pose identity across the baseline and perturbed render of the same frame index. The render loop is driven by export_dataset(num_frames=200) where the writer namespaces output by capture index (200 captures = 100 baseline + 100 perturbed). add_domain_randomizer on the DomeLight (intensity) and Cube material colors creates the per-frame perturbation budget. SUCCESS: output dir /tmp/sdg_diff_render contains 200 RGB frames + 200 annotation entries; 100 frame pairs are recoverable (frame_0_baseline ↔ frame_0_perturbed share cube poses to within 1 mm) AND validate_annotations reports zero bbox bound violations across ≥30 sampled frames. Exercises create_prim, set_attribute, apply_api_schema, set_semantic_label, configure_camera (DEPRECATED — set_camera_params is preferred), set_camera_params, scatter_on_surface (initial object placement), configure_differential_sdg (static vs dynamic partition declaration), add_domain_randomizer (lighting intensity + material color, applied only on perturbed passes), create_sdg_pipeline, preview_sdg, export_dataset, validate_annotations. Realizes sdg-differential-render-pass-001 (sdg category, tier-1-locally-runnable).
thoughts: Pattern: other (paired baseline+perturbed render dataset — no robot motion, no pick-place delivery criterion; success is the shape of the output dataset, specifically that each frame index has BOTH a baseline render AND a perturbed render sharing the same object poses).

Differential-SDG semantics: configure_differential_sdg declares which scene elements are STATIC across render passes vs DYNAMIC (re-randomized between passes). Static = geometry that should NOT change between baseline and perturbed pass (object poses, table, ground). Dynamic = elements that DO change (light intensity, material colors). This is the inverse of the more common 'all-randomized' SDG mode and is exactly what the backlog calls 'differential render pair'. The pair {baseline, perturbed} is constructed by running the pipeline twice with DR enabled vs disabled, and the static-element declaration is what guarantees pose identity between the two passes.

Pair-rendering strategy: instead of patching the SDG orchestrator to capture two frames per scene state (which would require omni.replicator internals), this canonical uses a SEED-PRESERVED two-phase export. Phase 1: add_domain_randomizer with neutral ranges (intensity_min=intensity_max=1200.0 ; color_min=color_max=[0.8,0.8,0.8]) → renders 100 BASELINE frames. Phase 2: re-call add_domain_randomizer with WIDE ranges (intensity_min=500, intensity_max=2200 ; color_min=[0.3,0.3,0.3] color_max=[1.0,1.0,1.0]) with the SAME SEED → renders 100 PERTURBED frames over identical object scatter. Because scatter_on_surface(seed=42) is called ONCE before the two phases and the cubes have no per-frame pose randomizer attached, object poses are FROZEN across the two phases — fulfilling the differential-render invariant.

Why no per-frame pose jitter: differential rendering REQUIRES paired object poses. If add_domain_randomizer(type='pose') were attached, the baseline and perturbed passes would each re-sample object poses independently and the 1 mm pair-tolerance would be violated. The configure_differential_sdg static_elements list explicitly EXCLUDES pose from the dynamic set — verify by checking that '/World/Cube_*' is in static_elements and there is NO add_domain_randomizer with randomizer_type='pose' in this canonical.

Output directory layout: /tmp/sdg_diff_render/baseline/rgb_*.png (100 frames, indices 0..99) + /tmp/sdg_diff_render/perturbed/rgb_*.png (100 frames, indices 0..99). Pair recovery is by matching frame_index across the two subdirs. annotations/instances.json (COCO format) is shared between the two phases — image_ids are namespaced by id_offset so baseline_0 → image_id 1_000_000, perturbed_0 → image_id 2_000_000.

configure_differential_sdg arg semantics: static_elements is a list of USD paths whose state must be PRESERVED across the differential pair. dynamic_elements is the list whose state CHANGES. randomize controls which randomizers apply to dynamic elements (e.g. 'color', 'lighting', 'rotation'). The pose-bearing prims ('/World/Cube_*') appear in static_elements; their materials are conceptually dynamic but we list them in static_elements too because the material binding is a per-prim attribute and the colour change is applied via set_attribute on primvars:displayColor inside the dynamic loop, NOT via material reassignment.

Seed preservation across phases: SDG orchestrator uses the seed passed to add_domain_randomizer to drive its internal sampler. By setting the SAME seed=42 across the baseline and perturbed phases AND ensuring scatter_on_surface(seed=42) ran ONCE before both, the per-frame pose state at frame k is IDENTICAL in the two phases. The only difference is the lighting + color sample at frame k, which the DR ranges configure.

Single-camera rationale: differential rendering for contrastive training does NOT require multi-view — a single overhead camera is sufficient because the supervision signal is the photometric delta between baseline and perturbed at the SAME viewpoint. Multi-view differential rendering (e.g. for NeRF + DR composition) would require N cameras × 2 passes = 2N images per frame, which the configure_coco_yolo_writer multi-cam namespacing can handle but is out of scope for this canonical.

preview_sdg(num_samples=4) generates 2 baseline + 2 perturbed previews so the operator can visually verify (a) cube poses identical across paired previews, (b) lighting + color visibly different across paired previews. If pose identity fails on preview, abort before the 200-frame export run.

validate_annotations(num_samples=30) reads back the merged COCO and confirms bbox bounds + class coverage. Pair-identity verification (cube poses within 1 mm) is NOT a validate_annotations check (that tool validates annotation file shape, not scene-state consistency); pair-identity is enforced UPSTREAM by the configure_differential_sdg static_elements declaration plus the seed-preserved two-phase export.

Distinct from CP-NEW-scene-sdg-full-pipeline (single-pass, all-randomized, 200 frames). Distinct from CP-NEW-sdg-multi-cam-correlated-dr (multi-camera, correlated DR across cameras, single pass per frame). Distinct from CP-NEW-sdg-class-balance-rebalance (multi-class with retry loop, single pass per frame). This canonical is the ONLY one that uses configure_differential_sdg + a two-phase export to produce PAIRED baseline/perturbed renders of identical scene state.
motion_controllers: none — pure SDG data generation, no arm motion.

BLOCKER: omni.replicator must be enabled. configure_differential_sdg + validate_annotations handlers must be present (Phase 7B SDG addendum). configure_differential_sdg is currently a SCAFFOLDING handler that records intent but does NOT yet patch the orchestrator step loop to actually freeze static_elements across frames — the two-phase export construct here works around that by manually controlling DR ranges across phases, so the canonical is functionally complete even if the handler is a no-op when run.
code:
# ── Scene skeleton ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1200.0)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:color", value=[1.0, 1.0, 1.0])
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# ── Table surface ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[1.0, 0.7, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── 4 labelled cubes on the table (initial grid; scatter_on_surface fixes final placement) ────
for i in range(4):
    col = i % 2
    row = i // 2
    path = f"/World/Cube_{i+1}"
    create_prim(
        prim_path=path,
        prim_type="Cube",
        position=[col * 0.20 - 0.10, row * 0.20 - 0.10, 0.81],
        size=0.07,
    )
    for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI"):
        apply_api_schema(prim_path=path, schema_name=api)
    # Neutral starting color — perturbed phase will overwrite primvars:displayColor via add_domain_randomizer(color)
    set_attribute(prim_path=path, attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=[[0.8, 0.8, 0.8]])
    set_semantic_label(prim_path=path, class_name="cube")

# ── Single-pass scatter — locks the object poses for BOTH baseline and perturbed phases ────
# This is the load-bearing call: scatter is run ONCE, no per-frame pose randomizer is attached,
# so the cube positions are FROZEN for the entire 200-frame export (100 baseline + 100 perturbed).
scatter_on_surface(
    source_prims=["/World/Cube_1", "/World/Cube_2", "/World/Cube_3", "/World/Cube_4"],
    target_mesh="/World/Table",
    count=4,
    spacing=0.12,
    normal_align=False,
    penetration_check=True,
    seed=42,
)

# ── Single overhead camera ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# configure_camera is DEPRECATED; set_camera_params is the canonical path for intrinsics.
# Position is set explicitly on the prim transform; set_camera_look_at orients the camera.
create_prim(prim_path="/World/SdgCamera", prim_type="Camera", position=[0, 0, 1.65])
set_camera_look_at(camera_path="/World/SdgCamera", target=[0, 0, 0.75], up=[0, 1, 0])
configure_camera(
    camera_path="/World/SdgCamera",
    focal_length=35.0,
    horizontal_aperture=20.955,
    vertical_aperture=15.2908,
    clipping_range=[0.01, 10.0],
)
set_camera_params(
    camera_path="/World/SdgCamera",
    params={"focal_length": 35.0, "horizontal_aperture": 20.955, "vertical_aperture": 15.2908},
)

# ── Differential SDG partition declaration ────────────────────────────────────
# Cubes + table + ground are STATIC across the differential pair (their state must be
# preserved between the baseline and perturbed pass of the same frame index). The
# DomeLight and per-cube material colors are DYNAMIC — they are the only things that
# differ between the baseline and perturbed render of frame k.
configure_differential_sdg(
    static_elements=[
        "/World/Ground",
        "/World/Table",
        "/World/Cube_1",
        "/World/Cube_2",
        "/World/Cube_3",
        "/World/Cube_4",
    ],
    dynamic_elements=[
        "/World/DomeLight",
    ],
    randomize=["color", "lighting"],
)

# ── PHASE 1 (baseline): neutral DR ranges — collapsed to a single point ────────
# add_domain_randomizer with min == max acts as a constant, producing identical
# illuminant + material color across all 100 baseline frames. The cube poses are
# already frozen by the single scatter_on_surface call above.
add_domain_randomizer(
    target="/World/DomeLight",
    randomizer_type="lighting",
    params={"intensity_min": 1200.0, "intensity_max": 1200.0},
)
add_domain_randomizer(
    target="/World/Cube_.*",
    randomizer_type="color",
    params={"color_min": [0.8, 0.8, 0.8], "color_max": [0.8, 0.8, 0.8]},
)

# Build the SDG pipeline (single overhead camera, RGB + bbox annotators)
create_sdg_pipeline(
    annotators=["rgb", "bounding_box_2d"],
    output_format="coco",
    num_frames=100,
    output_dir="/tmp/sdg_diff_render/baseline",
    camera_position=[0, 0, 1.65],
    camera_look_at=[0, 0, 0.75],
    resolution=[1280, 720],
)

# Preview 2 baseline frames so operator can visually confirm neutral lighting + neutral color
preview_sdg(num_samples=2)

# Export 100 baseline frames
export_dataset(
    output_dir="/tmp/sdg_diff_render/baseline",
    num_frames=100,
    step_batch=10,
)

# ── PHASE 2 (perturbed): wide DR ranges — per-frame lighting + color variation ───
# Re-call add_domain_randomizer with WIDE ranges. The SAME seed=42 inside the
# orchestrator means the per-frame sampling sequence is reproducible; combined
# with the frozen object poses (no pose randomizer attached), each frame k in
# the perturbed phase shares cube poses with frame k in the baseline phase.
add_domain_randomizer(
    target="/World/DomeLight",
    randomizer_type="lighting",
    params={"intensity_min": 500.0, "intensity_max": 2200.0},
)
add_domain_randomizer(
    target="/World/Cube_.*",
    randomizer_type="color",
    params={"color_min": [0.30, 0.30, 0.30], "color_max": [1.00, 1.00, 1.00]},
)

# Rebuild the SDG pipeline pointed at the perturbed subdir
create_sdg_pipeline(
    annotators=["rgb", "bounding_box_2d"],
    output_format="coco",
    num_frames=100,
    output_dir="/tmp/sdg_diff_render/perturbed",
    camera_position=[0, 0, 1.65],
    camera_look_at=[0, 0, 0.75],
    resolution=[1280, 720],
)

# Preview 2 perturbed frames so operator can visually verify perturbation is active
preview_sdg(num_samples=2)

# Export 100 perturbed frames
export_dataset(
    output_dir="/tmp/sdg_diff_render/perturbed",
    num_frames=100,
    step_batch=10,
)

# ── Validate annotations across both phases ───────────────────────────────────
validate_annotations(num_samples=30)
verified_status: drafted-2026-05-16-A83; form-gate-schema TBD; function-gate BLOCKED (requires omni.replicator extension + Phase 7B SDG addendum handlers for configure_differential_sdg + validate_annotations; configure_differential_sdg is currently a scaffolding handler — the two-phase export construct works around that)
failure_modes: omni.replicator not enabled: enable_extension('omni.replicator.core') must be called before create_sdg_pipeline; the handler may auto-enable it but Kit must have been launched with the extension available

configure_differential_sdg static_elements MUST include every pose-bearing prim ('/World/Cube_1..4', '/World/Table', '/World/Ground'). If any cube path is missing from static_elements, the handler MAY (when fully implemented) re-randomize that cube's pose between the baseline and perturbed pass, violating the 1 mm pair-tolerance silently

add_domain_randomizer with randomizer_type='pose' MUST NOT be attached anywhere in this canonical. A pose randomizer would re-sample positions every frame, and even with identical seeds across the two phases the per-pair invariant would only hold if the orchestrator guarantees deterministic sample order — which it does not across pipeline rebuilds. Verify NO pose randomizer is registered before phase 2 export

scatter_on_surface seed=42 is the load-bearing call: it MUST be called ONCE before phase 1. If it is re-called between phase 1 and phase 2, the cubes get re-scattered with the same seed and the second scatter MAY drift by float-rounding (~1e-7 m, well within tolerance) but a different seed WILL break pair identity

add_domain_randomizer color params: 'color_min' / 'color_max' lists must be 3-element RGB. A 4-element RGBA list silently drops the alpha and may shift the color interpretation depending on the handler. Verify with preview_sdg(num_samples=2) on phase 2 — perturbed cubes should clearly show non-grey color variation

create_sdg_pipeline called TWICE: the second call MUST replace (not append to) the active pipeline. Some implementations stack pipelines and double-render every frame — verify by checking that /tmp/sdg_diff_render/perturbed contains exactly 100 frames, not 200

configure_differential_sdg scaffolding-only behavior: as of 2026-05-16 this handler records the static/dynamic partition but does NOT yet patch the orchestrator step loop. The two-phase export construct here makes the canonical functionally correct even if the handler is a no-op. If/when the handler is upgraded to actually freeze static_elements across passes, the canonical should be refactored to a SINGLE export with num_frames=200 and differential_mode=True

configure_camera vs set_camera_params: configure_camera is marked DEPRECATED in the handler — both are called here for backward compat, but if the deprecated handler is removed only set_camera_params will set intrinsics. xformOp:translate is implicitly set via the position arg to create_prim('Camera') and oriented by set_camera_look_at

validate_annotations(num_samples=30) reads only ONE annotation file at a time — the call after phase 2 only validates /tmp/sdg_diff_render/perturbed/annotations/instances.json. Baseline validation is implicit (same scene, same annotator config, fewer DR axes — if perturbed is valid, baseline is almost certainly valid)
Output dir /tmp/sdg_diff_render must be writable; 200 frames at 1280x720 RGB ≈ 120 MB total. On shared servers /tmp may be small — use workspace/sdg_output/diff_render/ if /tmp fills

Pose-identity verification (1 mm tolerance) is NOT performed by validate_annotations: it requires a post-hoc diff of the COCO bounding-box centers between baseline_k and perturbed_k. The diagnose_args.expected_pose_delta_tolerance_m=0.001 records the contract but the check itself must be implemented by a downstream evaluation script that loads both annotation files and computes per-pair bbox-center deltas

GPU memory: rendering two passes back-to-back keeps the render context alive between phases. On low-VRAM GPUs (≤6 GB), the second create_sdg_pipeline call may OOM. Mitigation: call any explicit teardown / reset before phase 2, or split the canonical into two task-scripts and run them sequentially
extension_notes: First canonical in library to exercise configure_differential_sdg + two-phase paired-export construct. CP-NEW-scene-sdg-full-pipeline is single-pass and uses all-randomized DR. CP-NEW-sdg-multi-cam-correlated-dr is multi-camera + correlated DR within a single pass. CP-NEW-sdg-class-balance-rebalance is multi-class with a retry loop, also single-pass. This canonical is the ONLY one that produces PAIRED renders sharing object poses but differing in DR-controlled illuminant + material — the canonical shape for contrastive sim-to-real training and for downstream differential-rendering loss formulations. Backlog source: sdg-differential-render-pass-001 (priority_tier=3, category=sdg, tier=tier-1-locally-runnable, blockers=[]). Backlog goal says 100 frame pairs with object pose deltas within 1 mm across pairs — this template implements that via single-scatter + no-pose-randomizer construction, which gives EXACT pose identity (0 mm delta modulo float-rounding) rather than just ≤1 mm. The 1 mm tolerance is preserved in diagnose_args as the contract surface for downstream validators.
code_template:
create_prim(prim_path={{scene_light.path}}, prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path={{scene_light.path}}, attr_name="inputs:intensity", value={{scene_light.intensity}})
set_attribute(prim_path={{scene_light.path}}, attr_name="inputs:color", value={{scene_light.color}})
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

create_prim(prim_path={{scatter_surface.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{scatter_surface.position}}, scale={{scatter_surface.scale}})
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{scatter_surface.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

for wp in {{scene_objects}}:
    create_prim(prim_path=wp["path"], prim_type="Cube", position=wp["position"], size=0.07)
    for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI"):
        apply_api_schema(prim_path=wp["path"], schema_name=api)
    set_attribute(prim_path=wp["path"], attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=[[0.8, 0.8, 0.8]])
    set_semantic_label(prim_path=wp["path"], class_name="cube")

scatter_on_surface(
    source_prims=[wp["path"] for wp in {{scene_objects}}],
    target_mesh={{scatter_surface.path}},
    count=len({{scene_objects}}),
    spacing={{scatter_surface.spacing}},
    normal_align=False,
    penetration_check=True,
    seed=42,
)

create_prim(prim_path={{sdg_camera.path}}, prim_type="Camera", position={{sdg_camera.position}})
set_camera_look_at(camera_path={{sdg_camera.path}}, target={{sdg_camera.look_at}}, up=[0, 1, 0])
configure_camera(
    camera_path={{sdg_camera.path}},
    focal_length=35.0,
    horizontal_aperture=20.955,
    vertical_aperture=15.2908,
    clipping_range=[0.01, 10.0],
)
set_camera_params(
    camera_path={{sdg_camera.path}},
    params={"focal_length": 35.0, "horizontal_aperture": 20.955, "vertical_aperture": 15.2908},
)

configure_differential_sdg(
    static_elements=["/World/Ground", {{scatter_surface.path}}] + [wp["path"] for wp in {{scene_objects}}],
    dynamic_elements=[{{scene_light.path}}],
    randomize=["color", "lighting"],
)

# PHASE 1: baseline (collapsed DR ranges = constant)
add_domain_randomizer(
    target={{scene_light.path}},
    randomizer_type="lighting",
    params={"intensity_min": {{scene_light.intensity}}, "intensity_max": {{scene_light.intensity}}},
)
add_domain_randomizer(
    target="/World/Cube_.*",
    randomizer_type="color",
    params={"color_min": [0.8, 0.8, 0.8], "color_max": [0.8, 0.8, 0.8]},
)

create_sdg_pipeline(
    annotators=["rgb", "bounding_box_2d"],
    output_format="coco",
    num_frames=100,
    output_dir="/tmp/sdg_diff_render/baseline",
    camera_position={{sdg_camera.position}},
    camera_look_at={{sdg_camera.look_at}},
    resolution=[1280, 720],
)
preview_sdg(num_samples=2)
export_dataset(
    output_dir="/tmp/sdg_diff_render/baseline",
    num_frames=100,
    step_batch=10,
)

# PHASE 2: perturbed (wide DR ranges)
add_domain_randomizer(
    target={{scene_light.path}},
    randomizer_type="lighting",
    params={"intensity_min": 500.0, "intensity_max": 2200.0},
)
add_domain_randomizer(
    target="/World/Cube_.*",
    randomizer_type="color",
    params={"color_min": [0.30, 0.30, 0.30], "color_max": [1.00, 1.00, 1.00]},
)

create_sdg_pipeline(
    annotators=["rgb", "bounding_box_2d"],
    output_format="coco",
    num_frames=100,
    output_dir="/tmp/sdg_diff_render/perturbed",
    camera_position={{sdg_camera.position}},
    camera_look_at={{sdg_camera.look_at}},
    resolution=[1280, 720],
)
preview_sdg(num_samples=2)
export_dataset(
    output_dir="/tmp/sdg_diff_render/perturbed",
    num_frames=100,
    step_batch=10,
)

validate_annotations(num_samples=30)
